refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from mission context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Atomicity requires separating these concerns. CR-NGHE-005.1 focuses on the derivation activity—the transformation of mission inputs (worksite conditions, operational goals, constraints) into a set of quantified performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability). This is solution-neutral: it does not prescribe *how* derivation occurs (algorithm, tool, manual process) or *where* results are stored.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that given a defined worksite mission context (e.g., site layout, production target, safety constraints), the system produces a documented set of performance targets with clear traceability to mission inputs. Verify that targets are quantified and measurable.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements in a persistent, retrievable format.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with change timestamps and rationale.

candidate_dps: DP-NGHE-005.1a: Establish a parameterized performance derivation engine that ingests worksite mission context (operational scope, environmental constraints, safety policies) and applies a rule-based or model-based transformation to generate quantified performance targets with explicit traceability links to source inputs.

DP-NGHE-005.1b: Implement a hierarchical requirements decomposition process that maps worksite mission objectives to functional performance tiers, with each tier validated against operational policies and documented via a traceability matrix.

DP-NGHE-005.1c: Design a baseline management system that captures performance targets as versioned artifacts, records calibration/update events with timestamps and justification, and maintains an audit trail linking each baseline revision to worksite context changes.

DP-NGHE-005.1d: Adopt a federated context-capture pattern where worksite inputs (safety plan, operational policies, mission scope) are registered as external reference data sources, and performance derivation logic queries these sources on-demand to ensure real-time alignment without embedding context values.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-NGHE-005.1a + DP-NGHE-005.1c (integrated): A parameterized performance derivation engine that ingests worksite mission context and applies rule-based transformation to generate quantified performance targets, coupled with a versioned baseline management system that maintains a complete audit trail of performance target updates, calibration events, and traceability to source worksite inputs.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between derivation logic and baseline storage if both are monolithic; mitigated by separating derivation rules (stateless, reusable) from baseline persistence (versioned artifact store).

Dependency on external worksite context sources (safety plan, operational policies) introduces a data coupling; acceptable if interfaces are well-defined and context sources are treated as read-only reference data.

Traceability links must be bidirectional (target → source input and source input → dependent targets); risk of inconsistency if update propagation is not automated; recommend event-driven invalidation of dependent baselines.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP avoids prescribing specific derivation algorithms or storage technologies, maintaining solution neutrality. Separation of concerns (derivation vs. persistence vs. traceability) supports independent testing and evolution. Coupling to external context is necessary and acceptable if interfaces are contractual.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is available in a structured, queryable form (e.g., configuration database, document repository with metadata). RISK: If context is unstructured or scattered across multiple systems, derivation engine will require significant data integration effort. MITIGATION: Define a canonical worksite context schema early; validate availability during requirements phase.

ASSUMPTION: Performance targets can be derived deterministically from worksite context using rule-based or model-based logic. RISK: If derivation requires subjective judgment or domain expertise not easily codified, the system may produce targets that lack credibility or require manual override. MITIGATION: Involve domain experts in rule definition; include confidence/uncertainty metadata in derived targets.

ASSUMPTION: Baseline history and audit trail are required for compliance or operational continuity. RISK: If retention policies are not defined, the system may accumulate unbounded historical data, creating storage and query performance issues. MITIGATION: Define retention policies (e.g., keep last N baselines, archive older versions) and implement data lifecycle management.

ASSUMPTION: Traceability links are maintained automatically during derivation and update. RISK: If traceability is manual or post-hoc, links may become stale or incomplete, undermining the acceptance criterion. MITIGATION: Embed traceability capture in the derivation engine; validate completeness during baseline creation.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are stable or change infrequently. RISK: If policies change frequently, performance targets may become misaligned; the system must detect and flag such misalignments. MITIGATION: Implement change detection logic; trigger re-derivation or alert when context changes exceed a threshold.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR-NGHE-005.1 to explicitly state: (1) the expected frequency and scope of worksite context changes that trigger performance target re-derivation; (2) the acceptable latency between a context change and target update; (3) the required granularity of traceability (e.g., target ← policy clause, target ← operational constraint); (4) the definition of 'documented set' (e.g., format, schema, access controls). This will enable tighter DP specification and clearer acceptance criteria for IM design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized performance derivation engine and baseline management system that ingests worksite mission context, applies rule-based transformation logic, maintains versioned performance targets, and provides audit trail of all calibration and target updates.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context, environmental parameters, and operational state data required as inputs to the performance derivation engine.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Outputs quantified performance targets and derived operational parameters to tasking subsystems for execution.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and operational limits; outputs performance targets that respect safety boundaries.

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent containment. The DP is fundamentally a solution-level capability: parameterized derivation logic, rule-based transformation, and versioned baseline management are all core NGHE Solution responsibilities. Minimal diffusion: only interfaces to external telemetry inputs and tasking outputs; audit trail and versioning are entirely internal.

Poor containment. Vehicle module is a physical platform; performance derivation and baseline management are solution-level concerns, not vehicle-specific. Placing this DP in Vehicle would require Vehicle to own mission context interpretation, rule engines, and audit trails—severe architectural violation. High diffusion: logic would leak into teleoperation and safety services.

Poor containment. Teleoperation is a control/interface subsystem, not a derivation engine. Performance target generation is upstream of teleoperation; placing it here conflates control logic with strategic performance planning. High diffusion: would require teleoperation to own mission context parsing and baseline versioning, creating tight coupling to solution-level concerns.

Poor containment. Safety Services enforces safety constraints; it does not derive performance targets. While the DP must respect safety boundaries (via SafetyAlert interface), ownership of the derivation engine and baseline management belongs at solution level. Placing here would conflate safety enforcement with performance optimization logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The DP (parameterized performance derivation engine + versioned baseline management) is a solution-level architectural capability that synthesizes mission context into quantified operational targets. It is not a vehicle subsystem, a teleoperation function, or a safety enforcement mechanism. NGHE Solution is the only module with sufficient scope and responsibility to own both the rule-based transformation logic and the complete audit trail of performance target evolution. Containment is strong because the DP's inputs (worksite mission context via Telemetry) and outputs (performance targets via Tasking) align naturally with NGHE Solution's role as the orchestrator of mission-level decision-making.
containment_risks: Risk: If worksite mission context ingestion becomes highly vehicle-specific (e.g., terrain-dependent performance rules), there may be pressure to distribute rule evaluation logic into Vehicle module. Mitigation: Maintain clear separation—Vehicle provides raw telemetry; NGHE Solution interprets it via parameterized rules.

Risk: If baseline versioning and audit trail grow to support real-time performance tracking across multiple concurrent missions, the module may become a bottleneck. Mitigation: Design the baseline management system with scalable storage and query patterns; consider eventual consistency for audit log replication if needed.

Risk: Safety constraints may evolve dynamically during mission execution. If SafetyAlert interface becomes bidirectional with frequent constraint updates, coupling between NGHE Solution and Safety Services could increase. Mitigation: Define clear contract for SafetyAlert: NGHE Solution queries safety bounds at derivation time; Safety Services owns constraint enforcement.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH. Acceptance criteria explicitly require documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained history of baselines/calibration updates. The NGHE Solution IM boundary directly owns both: parameterized derivation engine produces traceable targets; versioned baseline management system maintains audit trail. Verification is straightforward: audit logs, target derivation records, and traceability matrices are all internal to the module and queryable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: EXCELLENT. FR-CR-NGHE-005.1 (derive performance requirements from worksite mission context) maps unambiguously to NGHE Solution IM. The module's scope explicitly includes mission context ingestion, rule-based transformation, and audit trail maintenance. No ambiguity about which module owns the capability. Worksite safety plan and operational policies flow in via SafetyAlert interface; NGHE Solution respects them during derivation but does not own them.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP (parameterized performance derivation engine + versioned baseline management) directly and sufficiently addresses FR (derive performance requirements from worksite mission context). The DP specifies the logical solution: rule-based transformation of mission context into quantified targets, coupled with complete audit trail. The DP is neither over-specified (does not prescribe implementation technology) nor under-specified (clearly defines transformation logic and versioning requirements). Coupling of derivation engine and baseline management is justified: both are required to satisfy the acceptance criterion of traceability and history maintenance.

diffusion_risks: TERRAIN-DEPENDENT RULE DIFFUSION: If worksite mission context interpretation becomes highly vehicle-specific (e.g., terrain-dependent performance rules, vehicle-specific payload constraints), there is risk that rule evaluation logic will be pressured to migrate into Vehicle module. Mitigation: Enforce strict separation—Vehicle provides raw telemetry (terrain type, payload mass, environmental conditions); NGHE Solution owns all rule-based interpretation and transformation. Parameterization of rules should be vehicle-agnostic at the DP level; vehicle-specific parameters are inputs, not logic.

BASELINE MANAGEMENT SCALABILITY BOTTLENECK: If baseline versioning and audit trail grow to support real-time performance tracking across multiple concurrent missions with high-frequency updates, the centralized NGHE Solution module may become a performance bottleneck or single point of failure. Risk of pressure to distribute audit logic into Telemetry or Tasking subsystems. Mitigation: Design baseline management with scalable storage (e.g., append-only log, eventual consistency for audit replication); implement query indexing on worksite context and timestamp; consider asynchronous audit trail writes if latency becomes critical.

SAFETY CONSTRAINT COUPLING: SafetyAlert interface is marked bidirectional (inout). If safety constraints evolve dynamically during mission execution with frequent updates, tight coupling between NGHE Solution and Safety Services could increase, risking diffusion of safety enforcement logic into performance derivation. Mitigation: Define clear contract—NGHE Solution queries safety bounds at derivation time (read-heavy); Safety Services owns constraint enforcement and updates. Establish versioning for safety constraints so NGHE Solution can audit which safety version was applied to each performance target.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: STRONG CONTAINMENT (0.92 score justified). The DP is fundamentally a solution-level orchestration capability: parameterized rule-based transformation and versioned baseline management are not vehicle subsystem concerns, not teleoperation control logic, and not safety enforcement mechanisms. NGHE Solution is the only module with sufficient scope to own both the derivation engine and the complete audit trail. Inputs (worksite mission context via Telemetry) and outputs (performance targets via Tasking) align naturally with NGHE Solution's role as mission-level decision orchestrator. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces are to Telemetry (input), Tasking (output), and SafetyAlert (constraint boundary). All core logic—rule evaluation, versioning, audit trail—remains internal. No evidence of scattered responsibilities or duplicated logic across module boundaries.

recommended_revisions: CLARIFY RULE PARAMETERIZATION BOUNDARY: Add explicit design note to DP-NGHE-005.1a specifying that rule parameters (e.g., performance scaling factors, mission context thresholds) are inputs to the derivation engine, not embedded logic. This prevents diffusion of rule logic into Vehicle or other subsystems. Recommend: 'Rule-based transformation shall be parameterized such that mission context interpretation logic is centralized in NGHE Solution; vehicle-specific and environment-specific parameters are provided as inputs via Telemetry interface.'

DEFINE SAFETY CONSTRAINT QUERY CONTRACT: Revise SafetyAlert interface specification to clarify directionality and timing. Recommend: 'NGHE Solution queries current safety constraints from Safety Services at performance derivation time (read operation). Safety Services updates constraints asynchronously; NGHE Solution logs which safety constraint version was applied to each performance target for audit traceability. No real-time constraint push from Safety Services during derivation.'

ADD BASELINE VERSIONING SCHEMA TO DP: Extend DP-NGHE-005.1c to specify versioning schema for baselines (e.g., semantic versioning, timestamp-based, mission-epoch-based). Recommend: 'Versioned baseline management system shall maintain immutable records of each performance target generation event, including: timestamp, source worksite mission context inputs, rule version applied, safety constraint version applied, and derived performance targets. All records shall be queryable by worksite ID, mission epoch, and timestamp range for audit trail reconstruction.'

ESTABLISH AUDIT TRAIL RETENTION AND QUERY PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS: Add acceptance criterion specifying audit trail retention period (e.g., mission duration + 90 days) and query latency SLA (e.g., retrieve audit trail for a worksite within 5 seconds). This prevents diffusion of audit logic into external systems and ensures NGHE Solution IM remains the authoritative source.

DOCUMENT WORKSITE MISSION CONTEXT SCHEMA: Create explicit specification of worksite mission context inputs (e.g., mission type, operational area, payload, environmental constraints, safety plan reference). This prevents ambiguity about what constitutes 'worksite mission context' and reduces risk of diffusion if context interpretation becomes ad-hoc.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence and strong containment (0.92). However, the recommended_revisions identify critical specification gaps that must be closed before proceeding to sub-FR decomposition. The safety criticality of this system (high) and the identified diffusion risks (terrain-dependent rule logic, baseline scalability, safety constraint coupling) require explicit design contracts and boundary clarifications at the current level. Proceeding to sub-FRs without these revisions risks: (1) diffusion of rule-based logic into Vehicle or Telemetry modules during detailed design, (2) ambiguity about safety constraint versioning and audit traceability, and (3) scalability bottlenecks in baseline management that could force architectural rework. The revisions are targeted, non-invasive refinements to DP and interface specifications—not fundamental re-architecture. Once revisions are incorporated, the FR-DP-IM structure is sufficiently mature for sub-FR decomposition.

revise.why: DP-NGHE-005.1 requires four targeted clarifications: (1) Explicit parameterization boundary for rule-based transformation to prevent diffusion of rule logic into Vehicle subsystem. (2) Safety constraint query contract specifying read-time semantics and versioning audit trail. (3) Baseline versioning schema with immutable record structure (timestamp, source inputs, rule version, safety constraint version, derived targets). (4) Audit trail retention and query performance SLA to establish NGHE Solution as authoritative source. These refinements strengthen the DP specification without changing the fundamental solution concept (parameterized derivation engine + versioned baseline management). They also directly address the three identified diffusion risks and support verification feasibility by making acceptance criteria more concrete and testable.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is well-defined and can be ingested via Telemetry interface without ambiguity.
Rule-based transformation logic can be fully parameterized and centralized in NGHE Solution without requiring vehicle-specific or terrain-specific branching logic.
Safety constraints are provided by Safety Services as queryable bounds (not as real-time push notifications) and can be versioned for audit trail purposes.
Baseline versioning and audit trail can be implemented with scalable storage (append-only log, eventual consistency) to support multi-mission concurrent execution without becoming a bottleneck.
Acceptance criteria for traceability and history maintenance can be operationalized as queryable audit logs with defined retention and latency SLAs.
open_risks: TERRAIN-DEPENDENT RULE DIFFUSION: If worksite mission context interpretation becomes highly vehicle-specific (e.g., terrain-dependent performance rules, vehicle-specific payload constraints), rule evaluation logic may be pressured to migrate into Vehicle module. Mitigation: Enforce strict separation—Vehicle provides raw telemetry; NGHE Solution owns all rule-based interpretation. Parameterization of rules must be vehicle-agnostic at DP level.

BASELINE MANAGEMENT SCALABILITY BOTTLENECK: If baseline versioning and audit trail grow to support real-time performance tracking across multiple concurrent missions with high-frequency updates, centralized NGHE Solution module may become a performance bottleneck or single point of failure. Mitigation: Design baseline management with scalable storage (append-only log, eventual consistency); implement query indexing; consider asynchronous audit trail writes if latency becomes critical.

SAFETY CONSTRAINT COUPLING: SafetyAlert interface is bidirectional (inout). If safety constraints evolve dynamically during mission execution with frequent updates, tight coupling between NGHE Solution and Safety Services could increase, risking diffusion of safety enforcement logic into performance derivation. Mitigation: Define clear contract—NGHE Solution queries safety bounds at derivation time (read-heavy); Safety Services owns constraint enforcement and updates. Establish versioning for safety constraints so NGHE Solution can audit which safety version was applied to each performance target.

AUDIT TRAIL INTEGRITY UNDER CONCURRENT MISSIONS: If multiple concurrent missions generate performance targets simultaneously, audit trail writes may race or become inconsistent. Mitigation: Implement atomic versioning (e.g., mission-epoch-based or timestamp-based with strict ordering); use immutable append-only log structure; validate audit trail consistency during integration testing.
evidence_needed: Explicit specification of worksite mission context schema (mission type, operational area, payload, environmental constraints, safety plan reference) to prevent ambiguity and ad-hoc interpretation.

Rule parameterization boundary document specifying which aspects of rule-based transformation are parameterized inputs vs. centralized logic, with examples of vehicle-agnostic vs. vehicle-specific parameters.

Safety constraint query contract specifying: (a) read-time semantics (NGHE Solution queries current constraints at derivation time), (b) versioning scheme (how safety constraint versions are tracked and logged), (c) audit trail structure (which safety version was applied to each performance target).

Baseline versioning schema specification including: immutable record structure, timestamp ordering, source input logging, rule version tracking, safety constraint version tracking, and derived target logging.
Audit trail retention and query performance SLA (e.g., retention period = mission duration + 90 days; query latency SLA = retrieve audit trail for a worksite within 5 seconds).
Scalability analysis for baseline management under concurrent multi-mission execution (e.g., expected audit trail growth rate, query frequency, storage requirements, latency targets).

Integration test plan validating: (1) rule parameterization boundary (no vehicle-specific logic in NGHE Solution), (2) safety constraint versioning and audit trail accuracy, (3) baseline versioning consistency under concurrent missions, (4) audit trail query performance against SLA.
